questions.text: People are a nation's greatest ________.
questions.explanation: Nature's bounty becomes significant only when people find it useful. It is people with their demands and abilities that turn them into 'resources'.

questions.text: The way in which people are spread across the earth surface is known as the ________.
questions.explanation: Pattern of population distribution refers to how people are spread across the world.

questions.text: More than 90 per cent of the world's population lives in about ________ per cent of the land surface.
questions.explanation: The distribution of population in the world is extremely uneven; most people live in a small fraction of the total land area.

questions.text: Almost three-quarters of the world's people live in two continents:
questions.explanation: Asia and Africa together house 75% of the world's population.

questions.text: Average density of population in the whole world is ________ persons per square km.
questions.explanation: According to the textbook data, the average density of the world is 51 persons per square km.

questions.text: Which country has the highest average density of population?
questions.explanation: South Central Asia has the highest density of population followed by East and South East Asia.

questions.text: The average density of population in India is ________ persons per square km.
questions.explanation: The average density of population in India is 382 persons per square km (based on 2011 census).

questions.text: Which of the following is a 'Topography' factor affecting population distribution?
options: People prefer living on plains rather than mountains
questions.explanation: Topography refers to the physical features of the land; plains like the Ganga plains are the most densely populated areas.

questions.text: The 'Diamond mines of South Africa' attract people due to which factor?
questions.explanation: Presence of mineral deposits makes an area attractive for settlement and industrial activity.

questions.text: Osaka in Japan and Mumbai in India are densely populated because they are:
questions.explanation: Industrial areas provide employment opportunities and thus attract large numbers of people.

questions.text: Population change refers to the change in the number of people during a ________.
questions.explanation: Population change is the difference in the number of inhabitants of a territory during a specific period of time.

questions.text: Birth rate is the number of live births per ________ people.
questions.explanation: Births are usually measured using the birth rate i.e. number of live births per 1,000 people.

questions.text: Natural Growth Rate is the difference between:
questions.explanation: The difference between the birth rate and the death rate of a country is called the natural growth rate.

questions.text: Migration is the movement of people ________.
options: In and out of an area
questions.explanation: Migration is the third way by which population size changes, involving people moving between locations.

questions.text: People who leave a country are called:
questions.explanation: Emigrants are people who leave a country; Immigrants are those who arrive in a country.

questions.text: The USA and Australia have gained in-numbers by ________.
questions.explanation: Countries like USA and Australia have seen population increases due to people moving into the country.

questions.text: A country that has experienced a loss in population numbers due to emigration is:
questions.explanation: Sudan is an example of a country that has experienced a loss in population due to out-migration or emigration.

questions.text: Population composition refers to the ________ of the population.
questions.explanation: Composition tells us about the age-sex ratio, literacy, health, and occupation of the people.

questions.text: A graphical method of investigating the population composition of a country is called ________.
questions.explanation: A population pyramid, also called an age-sex pyramid, shows the distribution of various age groups by sex.

questions.text: If a population pyramid has a very wide base, it means:
questions.explanation: A wide base indicates that a large number of children are born.

questions.text: The population pyramid of Kenya shows:
options: High birth rates and high death rates
Low birth rates and low death rates
High birth rates and low death rates
questions.explanation: Kenya's pyramid is broad at the base but narrows rapidly, indicating high births but many deaths at young ages.

questions.text: A population pyramid that is 'bulging' in the middle age groups indicates:
questions.explanation: In countries like India, lower death rates at younger ages mean more infants survive to adulthood, creating a large labor force.

questions.text: The population pyramid of Japan is narrow at the base, which indicates:
questions.explanation: Narrow base means low birth rates; decreased death rates allow more people to reach old age.

questions.text: Why is the population of the world increasing so rapidly today?
options: More people are being born than ever before
Death rates have fallen due to better food and medicine
questions.explanation: The main reason for the 'population explosion' is the dramatic fall in death rates while birth rates remained high.

questions.text: Life expectancy is the number of years that ________.
options: A person is expected to work
An average person can expect to live
A person lives in one country
questions.explanation: It is a statistical measure of the average time an organism is expected to live.

questions.text: People in the age group of 0-14 and above 65 are considered as:
questions.explanation: They are dependent on the working population (15-64 years) for their needs.

questions.text: Which of these regions is very sparsely populated?
questions.explanation: Extreme climates like hot deserts (Sahara) or cold regions are sparsely populated.

questions.text: Which religious/cultural factor attracts people to Varanasi or Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Places with religious or cultural significance attract people for settlement.

questions.text: In 1999, the world population reached ________.
questions.explanation: The world population reached 6 billion in 1999, doubling from 3 billion in just 30 years.

questions.text: Population density is usually measured as number of people per ________.
questions.explanation: Density is people per unit area of land, standardized to square kilometers.

questions.text: The movement of people from rural to urban areas is mainly in search of:
questions.explanation: Urban areas provide more opportunities, leading to rural-to-urban migration.

questions.text: What is the primary goal of the Ministry of Human Resource Development in India?
questions.explanation: The HRD Ministry (now Ministry of Education) was created to improve the quality of people's skills.

questions.text: The fertility of soil is a ________ factor affecting population distribution.
questions.explanation: Soil, water, climate and topography are geographical factors.

questions.text: A 'stationary' population pyramid, where the top and base are almost the same, indicates:
questions.explanation: When birth and death rates are both low, the population becomes stable.

questions.text: Which country is an example of a population that is very large but not necessarily economically productive?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh and Japan are both densely populated, but Japan is more economically developed because of the skill level of its human resources.

questions.text: The 'Pull' factor in migration means:
options: People are attracted to a new location
questions.explanation: Pull factors like better jobs or safety attract people to a region.

questions.text: Most of the world's population lives in the ________ regions.
questions.explanation: Moderate climates are preferred over extreme cold or heat.

questions.text: The global population is expected to reach ________ by 2050 (estimate in some contexts).
questions.explanation: Projections suggest the population will continue to grow, hitting 9 billion around mid-century.

questions.text: Skilled, spirited and hopeful young people are the ________ of any nation.
questions.explanation: Human resource development focuses on empowering the youth to lead the nation.

questions.text: Birth rate is high and death rate is high results in a ________ pyramid.
questions.explanation: High births create a wide base, and high deaths cause it to narrow quickly toward the top.
